species.notes: Archive-backed domestication projects are known, but species-level runtime ingestion is not yet implemented.

species_manifest.scientific_scope: Species-aware ancient-DNA intake and review boundary. Support status describes evidence readiness, not broad scientific sufficiency.
species_manifest.runtime_scope: Current runtime normalization and reporting remain human AADR-first until species-specific collectors and archive bridges are implemented.

coordinate_provenance_records.original_place_text: Botai culture of the Central Asian steppes
coordinate_provenance_records.confidence_rationale: The archived paper names Botai explicitly, but does not ship a direct coordinate pair in the local source archive, so the current coordinate is an approximate named-site geocode.
coordinate_provenance_records.interpretation_note: Botai remains the only clearly named domesticated-core horse site that currently earns point-level publication.

site_evidence_records.exact_source_text: The Eneolithic Botai culture of the Central Asian steppes provides the earliest archaeological evidence for horse husbandry, ~5500 years ago, but the exact nature of early horse domestication remains controversial.
site_evidence_records.interpretation_note: Botai is the current horse anchor because the paper explicitly states the husbandry context and the repo already maps it at site-level resolution.

locality_records.nordic_inclusion_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
locality_records.interpretation_note: Botai remains the only clearly named domesticated-core horse site that currently earns point-level publication.

project_summaries.nordic_relevance_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
project_summaries.interpretation_caveat: Archive-backed horse dataset kept provisional until explicit primary-paper linkage is recorded. Keep this project out of strong pollenomics interpretation until the primary paper linkage is encoded explicitly. This project stays in the evidence base for comparative context, not as shipped Nordic evidence.
project_summaries.notes: Archive-backed horse dataset kept provisional until explicit primary-paper linkage is recorded.

normalization_scope: Non-human normalization currently governs accession-backed sample master rows, project summaries, study summaries, and curated locality summaries. Site and chronology extraction still vary by accession and remain explicit in sample inclusion status rather than being silently flattened.
